trans.context: क्या आपको कोई spare part चाहिए या कोई और मदद?
trans.explain: The user asked about a spare part; the ASR wrote "self part". One word, but it changes what the customer wants — downstream the agent searched the wrong thing. This is exactly the judgment ASR can't do: "self" and "spare" sound close on a phone line, the meaning doesn't.

बस एक मिनट दीजिए, मैं अभी वापस आती हूँ।
Great - मैंने checkout link आपके WhatsApp पर भेज दिया है। क्या मैं आपकी और किसी चीज़ में मदद कर सकती हूँ?
नहीं बस यही वाला order करना था
धन्यवाद! जीना सीखो के साथ खरीदारी का आनंद लें - आपका दिन शुभ हो।

anchors.text: नहीं बस यही वाला order करना था

trans.context: इस order का total ₹100 है। कितने का order करना चाहेंगे?
trans.explain: The user said "कितना... one hundred" — the amount got dropped by the transcript. One reviewer even marked this segment correct; the experts caught it because the number the user gives IS the order. Numbers, names and amounts are where a missed word costs money.

I am Zara from RENÉE Cosmetics. Were you looking to buy Hot Shot Face and Body Glowstick? We noticed you left it in your cart.
yeah it has some rooms also
Okay got it, so
what colors is it
what colors do you have
The Hot Shot Glowstick is mainly a glossy, natural glow shade for cheeks and body, no co
ok yeah i would like but on cash only when it is delivered i can pay cash

I’m not able to edit your cart. I've sent the checkout link with your current items, you can add or change anything directly on the website. Shall I send the checkout link with your 10percent discount to your WhatsApp?
Hey, are you still there

anchors.text: yeah it has some rooms also
ok yeah i would like but on cash only when it is delivered i can pay cash

trans.context: This Zara satin midi dress is still in your cart. Would you like to complete the order?
trans.explain: This one was clean — the ASR heard it right. Half the skill is NOT flagging good segments: marking correct ASR as wrong wastes expert time and poisons the golden dataset. Trust your ears, not your suspicion.

turns.text: Hi. I’m Isha, an AI agent from iGolf. Due to limited availability, we are currently accepting advance bookings online on our website from Monday to Thursday. You're welcome to visit as a walk-in on weekends, subject to availability. How may I assist you today?
i want to book

I can help you with that! However, please note that we are currently accepting advance bookings online from Monday to Thursday. You can make your booking through our website at igolfpune.com. If you're looking to visit on a weekend, you're welcome to walk in, subject to availability. Is there anything else I can assist you with?
i want to talk with someone
Please give me a moment. I will transfer the call to a human agent.

anchors.text: i want to talk with someone

pron.explain: The agent garbles the city — "Pune" comes out as "pune.com", like a website. City and proper-noun slots are read from text the TTS has never heard aloud, so they break in ways only a listener catches. Tag = City so the fix lands in the right pronunciation dictionary.

turns.text: Hello, am i speaking with Manali?
मैं ज़ारा हूँ RENÉE Cosmetics से। क्या आप Pink Therapy Anti Aging Skincare Combo खरीदना चाहती थीं? आपका item cart में है।

pron.explain: The agent mispronounces the customer's name, Manali, in the very first line. Names are the highest-stakes pronunciation slot — get it wrong and the customer stops trusting the call in the first five seconds. Tag = Proper Noun.

anchors.text: यह problem है ai से call कराने की

issue.setup: Play from the marked moment. Listen to what the agent does right after the user goes quiet — it reacts to something the user never said, then stalls. Log the issue the way you would in the tool.
issue.explain: The agent captured background noise as user input and paused mid-flow — a User input capture error. It's not a wrong response (the response followed from what it THOUGHT it heard) and not repetition. Picking the right type matters because input-capture errors route to the ASR team, not the prompt team.
